req.title: Read patient (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-052 defines read access to /Patient/{id} for clinicians. The positive path (EP) validates that an assigned clinician receives 200 and an audit entry. The negative path (NEG) validates that an unassigned clinician receives 403. The boundary path (BVA) targets the edge between assigned and unassigned by using adjacent patient IDs at the exact boundary of the clinician's care list. A Decision Table (DT) negative case covers a nonexistent patient ID returning 404. The state-transition case (STC) is classified as positive because it exercises the happy-path state progression idle->reading->audit-written, confirming the audit post-condition is written after a successful read; STC is not appropriate for a negative kind per rule constraints, so the kind is corrected to positive here while the test logic is preserved intact.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: Auth token for 'clinician_assigned' is obtainable via <get_auth_token>
actions: Capture HTTP status and response body
Query audit log for the read event
expected: Response body id matches requested patient id
Audit log contains one entry for this read with outcome success
trace: R-HLT-052: clinician shall read patient resource for patients in their care
R-HLT-052: access audit written on successful read
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit(
    assigned_clinician_token, assigned_patient_id, api_client, audit_log_client
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert body.get("id") == assigned_patient_id

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        actor_id="clinician_assigned",
        resource_id=assigned_patient_id,
        action="read"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    assert audit_entries[-1]["outcome"] == "success"

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403_no_audit_success
preconditions: Auth token for 'clinician_unassigned' is obtainable via <get_auth_token>
actions: Capture HTTP status and response body
Query audit log for any success entry for this read
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue[0].code == 'forbidden'
Audit log does NOT contain a success entry for this read attempt
trace: R-HLT-052: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
R-HLT-052: error pathway 403 when clinician not assigned
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403_no_audit_success(
    unassigned_clinician_token, unassigned_patient_id, api_client, audit_log_client
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {unassigned_clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("code") == "forbidden" for i in issues)

    success_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        actor_id="clinician_unassigned",
        resource_id=unassigned_patient_id,
        action="read",
        outcome="success"
    )
    assert len(success_entries) == 0

tests.name: test_boundary_last_assigned_vs_first_unassigned_patient_id
preconditions: Patient 'boundary_assigned_id' is the last patient in clinician's assignment list
Patient 'boundary_unassigned_id' is the immediately adjacent patient NOT in the list
Both patient resources exist in the system
expected: GET boundary_assigned_id returns HTTP 200 with matching Patient resource
GET boundary_unassigned_id returns HTTP 403 with OperationOutcome
trace: R-HLT-052: access boundary between assigned and unassigned patients
R-HLT-052: BVA on care assignment partition edge
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_boundary_last_assigned_vs_first_unassigned_patient_id(
    boundary_clinician_token,
    boundary_assigned_patient_id,
    boundary_unassigned_patient_id,
    api_client
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {boundary_clinician_token}"}

    resp_in = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{boundary_assigned_patient_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_in.status_code == 200
    assert resp_in.json().get("id") == boundary_assigned_patient_id

    resp_out = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{boundary_unassigned_patient_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert resp_out.status_code == 403
    body_out = resp_out.json()
    assert body_out.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_nonexistent_patient_id_returns_404
preconditions: Patient ID 'nonexistent-patient-id-99999' does not exist in the system
trace: R-HLT-052: missing mandatory elements error pathway
R-HLT-052: DT row — valid auth, valid role, invalid resource id
tests.executable:
import pytest

NONEXISTENT_ID = "nonexistent-patient-id-99999"

def test_nonexistent_patient_id_returns_404(
    assigned_clinician_token, api_client
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{NONEXISTENT_ID}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("code") == "not-found" for i in issues)

tests.name: test_audit_written_only_after_successful_read_state_transition
preconditions: No prior audit entries exist for this clinician+patient pair in this test run
A unique correlation ID is generated per test invocation to isolate audit queries
actions: Query audit log BEFORE making any read request — assert zero entries for this correlation
Query audit log AFTER successful read — assert exactly one new entry with outcome success
expected: Audit entry count is 0 before the read request
Audit entry count is exactly 1 after the read request with outcome == 'success'
State has transitioned from idle to audit-written confirming post-condition
trace: R-HLT-052: access audit written — state transition from pre-read (idle) to post-read (audit-written)
R-HLT-052: STC covering idle -> reading -> audit-written states
R-HLT-052: positive path confirming audit post-condition is satisfied after successful read
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_audit_written_only_after_successful_read_state_transition(
    assigned_clinician_token, assigned_patient_id,
    api_client, audit_log_client
):
    correlation_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

    # State: idle — no audit entry should exist yet
    pre_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        actor_id="clinician_assigned",
        resource_id=assigned_patient_id,
        action="read",
        correlation_id=correlation_id
    )
    assert len(pre_entries) == 0, "Expected zero audit entries before read"

    # Transition: idle -> reading -> audit-written
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}",
            "X-Correlation-ID": correlation_id
        }
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    # State: audit-written — exactly one entry must now exist
    post_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(
        actor_id="clinician_assigned",
        resource_id=assigned_patient_id,
        action="read",
        correlation_id=correlation_id
    )
    assert len(post_entries) == 1, "Expected exactly one audit entry after successful read"
    assert post_entries[0]["outcome"] == "success"
